revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:はざみ山古墳]]
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Hazamiyama Kofun |image=File:Hazamiyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} |location={{ill|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|1=Fujiidera}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.561667|N|135.602222|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped Kofun]] |area=Mound length: 103m |Burial Facility= |built=Mid-5th century |Interred Persons= |burial goods= |cultural property=Included in the National Historic Site Historic Site of {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}} |architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} }}
[[File:851015 Hazami & Miyayama aerial.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|'''Hazamiyama Kofun''' (top) and {{ill|Nonaka Miyama Kofun|ja|野中宮山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonaka Miyama Kofun|qq=}} (bottom) in an aerial photograph}}{{small|{{国土航空写真}}.}}]]
'''Hazamiyama Kofun''' (はざみやまこふん) is an ancient Kofun in {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera|1=Fujiidera}}, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is a keyhole-shaped kofun and part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Cluster|1=Furuichi Kofun Cluster}}, Historic Site1 as a {{ill|World Heritage Site|en|World Heritage Site|qid=Q9259|lt=World Heritage Site|1=World Heritage Site}}. The kofun is a National Historic Site under the Historic Site "Furuichi Kofun Cluster."
Historically, the kanji "挾山" was used to refer to the site, but today it is more commonly written in hiragana. It is sometimes referred to as "Hasamiyama."
== Overview ==
The front portion of the mound faces east and is surrounded by a single moat. The mound measures 103 meters in length. Portions of the rear circular section's moat have been filled in, and some of the land has become privately owned. Additionally, the front portion borders construction on {{ill|Japan National Route 170|en|Japan National Route 170|qid=Q6156852|lt=Japan National Route 170|1=Japan National Route 170}} (Osaka Outer Ring Road) conducted in 1974. Addroximately 100 meters to the south lies the {{ill|Nonaka Miyama Kofun|ja|野中宮山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonaka Miyama Kofun|qq=}}, with a mound length of 154 meters.
Surrounding these kofun is the {{ill|Hasamiyama Site|ja|はさみ山遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasamiyama Site|qq=}}, where excavations have revealed evidence of large settlements, including post holes and drainage ditches, dating from the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|qid=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}} to the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|qid=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period|1=Muromachi period}}.
<gallery> File:Hazamiyama Kofun, funcho.jpg|Summit of the rear circular section File:Hazamiyama Kofun, kouenbu.jpg|View from the front portion toward the rear circular section File:Hazamiyama Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|View from the rear circular section toward the front portion </gallery>
== Land Ownership Dispute ==
In 1990, part of the kofun's rear moat, approximately 1,154.65 square meters of private land, became the focus of a development permit addlication denied by Osaka Prefecture. The landowners subsequently registered the property as co-owned among four parties, including a construction company, and requested Osaka Prefecture to purchase the land. In 1991, Fujiidera City (via its Land Development Corporation) purchased the land for approximately 870 million yen.<ref name=人民日報>[http://j.people.com.cn/2004/09/20/print20040920_43543.html "National Historic Site Kofun Put Up for Auction in Osaka"] {{ill|People's Daily|en|People's Daily|qid=Q54340|lt=People's Daily|1=People's Daily}}, September 20, 2004.</ref>
In 1996, the Hazamiyama Kofun was Historic Site1 as a National Historic Site. However, in 1999, a legal dispute arose regarding a removed mortgage lien on part of the property. The Osaka District Court ruled in favor of the lienholder, leading to an unprecedented situation in 2004 where part of the kofun was auctioned.<ref name=人民日報/><ref>"Kofun Auction Raises Controversy in Osaka," {{ill|The Asahi Shimbun|en|The Asahi Shimbun|qid=Q720503|lt=Asahi Shimbun|1=The Asahi Shimbun}}, September 20, 2020.</ref>
In January 2005, a real estate company in Sakai City purchased the land at auction for 8.88 million yen but faced restrictions preventing development. Subsequent negotiations with Fujiidera City failed. Legal proceedings in 2006 resulted in the court ordering the company to sell the land to the city for 3.7 million yen.
== Transportation Access ==
* {{ill|Minami Osaka Line|en|Minami Osaka Line|qid=Q901342|lt=Kintetsu Minami Osaka Line|1=Minami Osaka Line}} {{ill|Fujiidera Station|en|Fujiidera Station|qid=Q5507418|lt=Fujiidera Station|1=Fujiidera Station}}: Transfer to a {{ill|Kintetsu Bus|en|Kintetsu Bus|qid=Q18636319|lt=Kintetsu Bus|1=Kintetsu Bus}} and alight at Nonaka Bus Stop.
== References ==
{{Reflist}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Hasamiyama Kofun}} * [https://www.city.fujiidera.lg.jp/rekishikanko/shiteibunkazai/kunifusiteibunkazai/1387762354938.html Fujiidera City Designated Historic Site - Hazamiyama Kofun] * {{PDFlink|[http://www.occh.or.jp/static/pdf/data/setumeikai/h15_052.pdf Excavation Report of Hazamiyama Kofun]}} - Osaka Prefecture Historic Site Center
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{translated page|ja|はざみ山古墳|version=104043914|comment=Imported full ja history}}
